scenario: An IDP extracts data with per-field confidence. The team wonders about computing an overall confidence for the entire extraction -- e.g., to compare across documents.
question: How might "overall confidence" be computed from per-field confidences?

options.text: Options (using canonical FEEL projection for ... in ... return ... , vendorName matters more than memo). Choice depends on the use case. " → minimum is safest. ") → mean.

The IDP component automatically computes and returns an overall confidence score with no need for manual aggregation -- some IDP extraction responses may include aggregate confidence metrics depending on the implementation and version.

Overall confidence is not a meaningful or useful metric when working with IDP extraction results -- this dismisses a valuable quality indicator. Aggregating per-field confidence scores into an overall confidence metric helps teams decide whether to auto-process a document or route it for human review. A minimum confidence threshold check prevents silent errors from low-quality extractions reaching downstream systems without appropriate human validation.

Mean confidence masks individual weak fields that may contain critical data errors. " decisions, while weighted averages allow business-critical fields like vendor name and invoice amount to carry more weight than secondary fields. The correct aggregation method depends on the specific use case.

a.text: Correct. "Overall confidence" can be computed in FEEL from per-field confidences. Pick the aggregation method based on semantic: Minimum (conservative): =min(for f in fields return f.confidence) -- canonical FEEL projection per feel-list-expressions.md:67-86. Use case: "is the extraction trustworthy?" -- only as good as the weakest field. Highlights potential issues. Mean (general feel): =mean(for f in fields return f.confidence) Use case: aggregate quality reporting; masks individual weak fields. Weighted average (importance-aware): =(0.5 vendorName.confidence + 0.3 invoiceDate.confidence + 0.2 * amount.confidence) Use case: business-critical fields (vendorName, amount) weighted more than secondary (memo). Median (robust): =median(for f in fields return f.confidence) Use case: less sensitive to outliers than mean. Anti-pattern: bracket-comprehension [f in fields : f.confidence] looks plausible but is NOT FEEL syntax -- projection uses for ... in ... return ...; the bracket form list[predicate] is filter syntax with implicit item variable per :75. For "should I auto-process this?" decision: minimum is typically safest -- if any critical field is low-confidence, don't auto-process. For trend analytics, mean or median.

b.text: Incorrect. Some IDP responses may include aggregate; check.
